content.subtitle: Form
items: I / you / he / she / it / we / they

content.subtitle: Use
items: A subject pronoun tells us who or what performs the action of the verb.

content.subtitle: Example
items: Look at those children! **They** are playing football.
Maria loves baking, and **she** makes the best cakes in town.

content.subtitle: Form
items: me / you / him / her / it / us / them

content.subtitle: Use
items: As the object of a verb (receives the action): The teacher called **him** to the front.
After prepositions: Could you send the documents to **us** by Friday?

content.subtitle: Form
items: my / your / his / her / its / our / their

content.subtitle: Use
items: To indicate who something belongs to. They always come before a noun: I left **my** keys on the table.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: A possessive determiner must always be followed by a noun — you cannot use it alone.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: The cat licked **its** paw. (= the cat's paw — possessive determiner)
**It's** a lovely day today. (= It is ... — contraction)

content.subtitle: Form
items: mine / yours / his / hers / ours / theirs

content.subtitle: Use
items: To talk about ownership without repeating the noun: I forgot my umbrella. Can I borrow **yours**?

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Unlike possessive determiners, possessive pronouns stand alone — they are never followed by a noun.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: There is no possessive pronoun for **it**.

content.subtitle: Form
items: myself / yourself / himself / herself / itself / ourselves / yourselves / themselves

content.subtitle: Use
items: When the subject and the object of the verb are the same person or thing: She hurt **herself** while cycling.
To add emphasis — to stress that someone did something without any help: We painted the whole house **ourselves**!

sections.title: A: Subject pronouns
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences with a subject pronoun.

items.sentence: Sofia and I are hungry. ...... want some lunch.
Where is Leo? ...... is upstairs.
My tablet is old. ...... doesn't work very well.
Hugo and Clara are cousins. ...... live in Valencia.
I'm not ready, but ...... can leave in five minutes.
Mia, are ...... coming with us?

sections.title: B: Object pronouns
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences with an object pronoun.

items.sentence: Could you call ...... after school?
I sent Marta a message, but she didn't read ...... .
We met Alex and gave ...... the address.
The plants are dry. Please water ...... .
My cousins are visiting, so I'm showing ...... the city.
Please tell ...... the answer when you know it.
Is this **mine / my** coat?
Laura can't find **her / hers** phone.
The bird is cleaning **it's / its** wings.
We are visiting **ours / our** grandparents tomorrow.
The children forgot **their / theirs** bags.
I really like **yours / your** idea.

sections.title: D: Possessive pronouns
sections.instructions: Complete with the correct possessive pronoun.

items.sentence: This pen belongs to me. It's ...... .
That scarf belongs to Julia. It's ...... .
These seats are for you. They're ...... .
The small garden belongs to our house. It's ...... .
Those bikes belong to my neighbours. They're ...... .
This watch belongs to my father. It's ...... .
Don't touch the oven. You might burn ...... .
I made this shelf ...... .
They cleaned the garage ...... .
We organised the party ...... .
My grandmother taught ...... to use a smartphone.
The door closes by ...... .
Laura and I are neighbours. ...... walk to school together.
I can't find my glasses. Have you seen ...... ?
That suitcase isn't mine. It's ...... .
Oscar cooked dinner by ...... .
My sister and I share ...... bedroom.
Where is Carla? I need to speak to ...... .

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one pronoun mistake. Write the correct word.

items.sentence: This is **theirs** house.
**Him** is my cousin.
Can you explain this to **we**?
The boys repaired the bike by **himself**.
The dog is sleeping in **it's** basket.
Those notebooks are **your**.

sections.title: H: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one pronoun mistake. Find the error and write the whole sentence correctly.

items.sentence: That umbrella is **her**.
Leo fixed the computer by **him**.
items.answer: Leo fixed the computer by himself.

items.sentence: This is **we** classroom.
Could you show **they** the photos?
items.answer: Could you show them the photos?

items.sentence: The blue bag is **she**.
They prepared everything by **them**.

sections.title: I: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
Anna wrote the story without help from anyone.
Anna wrote the story ......

The wallet belongs to David.
That is ...... .

My sister cut herself while cooking.
My sister ...... while cooking.

We cleaned the whole kitchen without any help.
We cleaned the whole kitchen ...... .

Does the black backpack belong to you?
...... black backpack?

The students created the posters on their own.
The students created ...... .
